meta.name: SELinux Testcases - Android + Linux
notes: Run these commands on the target shell. For Android, typically prefix with: adb shell '<command>'.
Checks are designed to minimize false positives: only a few are strict pass/fail; most are evidence collectors.
Some evidence checks may require root (noted per check).

checks.label: SELinux mode is Enforcing (authoritative)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v getenforce >/dev/null 2>&1; then getenforce 2>/dev/null; elif [ -r /sys/fs/selinux/enforce ]; then v=$(cat /sys/fs/selinux/enforce 2>/dev/null); [ "$v" = "1" ] && echo Enforcing || echo Permissive; else echo Permissive; fi'
checks.description: Fail if SELinux is not enforcing. getenforce explicitly reports enforcing/permissive/disabled.

checks.label: Kernel cmdline does not disable SELinux
checks.command: sh -c 'if [ -r /proc/cmdline ] && grep -Eq "(androidboot\.selinux=permissive|selinux=0|enforcing=0)" /proc/cmdline 2>/dev/null; then echo BAD; else echo OK; fi'
checks.description: Fails only if explicit disable/permissive flags are present in /proc/cmdline.

checks.label: SELinux enable file exists and shows enforcing bit (1)
checks.command: sh -c 'if [ -r /sys/fs/selinux/enforce ]; then cat /sys/fs/selinux/enforce 2>/dev/null; else echo NO_FILE; fi'
checks.description: Checks /sys/fs/selinux/enforce (1=enforcing, 0=permissive). Missing file suggests SELinux not mounted/enabled.

checks.label: SELinux policy is present (policy file non-empty)
checks.command: sh -c 'if [ -s /sys/fs/selinux/policy ]; then echo PRESENT; else echo MISSING; fi'
checks.description: Verifies that a policy blob is loaded (non-empty policy file).

checks.label: selinuxenabled utility reports enabled (if available)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v selinuxenabled >/dev/null 2>&1; then selinuxenabled >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?; [ $rc -eq 0 ] && echo ENABLED || echo DISABLED; else echo NO_TOOL; fi'
checks.description: If selinuxenabled exists, it exits 0 when enabled. If missing, it does not fail.

checks.label: sestatus reports enabled + enforcing (if available)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v sestatus >/dev/null 2>&1; then (sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "SELinux status: *enabled" && sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "Current mode: *enforcing") && echo ENFORCING || echo NOT_ENFORCING; else echo NO_TOOL; fi'
checks.description: Uses sestatus when present to confirm enabled+enforcing. If sestatus is missing, it does not fail.

checks.label: Linux config requests enforcing (if config file exists)
checks.command: sh -c 'if [ -r /etc/selinux/config ]; then v=$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*SELINUX=" /etc/selinux/config | tail -n 1 | tr -d "\r" ); echo "$v"; else echo NO_FILE; fi'
checks.description: If /etc/selinux/config exists, flags permissive/disabled. Missing file does not fail.

checks.label: selinuxfs appears mounted
checks.command: sh -c 'grep -Eq "\bselinuxfs\b" /proc/mounts 2>/dev/null && echo MOUNTED || echo NOT_MOUNTED'
checks.description: If SELinux is active, selinuxfs is typically mounted.

checks.label: Current shell has an SELinux context (id -Z)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v id >/dev/null 2>&1; then id -Z 2>/dev/null || echo NO_CONTEXT; else echo NO_ID; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: prints SELinux context for current identity.

checks.label: Process contexts available (ps -eZ)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v ps >/dev/null 2>&1; then ps -eZ 2>/dev/null | head -n 15; else echo NO_PS; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: lists processes with contexts (first 15 lines).

checks.label: getenforce present
checks.command: sh -c 'command -v getenforce >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PRESENT || echo MISSING'
checks.description: Evidence: confirms getenforce tool availability.

checks.label: setenforce present (and not blocked)
checks.command: sh -c 'command -v setenforce >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PRESENT || echo MISSING'
checks.description: Evidence: confirms setenforce tool availability (does not change mode).

checks.label: getsebool available (Linux SELinux booleans)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v getsebool >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo PRESENT; getsebool -a 2>/dev/null | head -n 20; else echo MISSING; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: booleans exist and are readable (first 20 lines).

checks.label: semodule list available (installed policy modules)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v semodule >/dev/null 2>&1; then semodule -l 2>/dev/null | head -n 30; else echo NO_SEMODULE; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: shows loaded SELinux modules (first 30 lines).

checks.label: Android: count SELinux denials in logcat (avc:)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v logcat >/dev/null 2>&1; then c=$(logcat -b all -T 1000 -d 2>/dev/null | grep -ci "avc:"); echo "AVC_LOGCAT_COUNT=$c"; else echo NO_LOGCAT; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: counts 'avc:' denials in logcat.

checks.label: Android: count SELinux denials in dmesg (avc:)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v dmesg >/dev/null 2>&1; then c=$(dmesg 2>/dev/null | grep -ci "avc:"); echo "AVC_DMESG_COUNT=$c"; else echo NO_DMESG; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: counts 'avc:' denials in dmesg.

checks.label: Android: previous-boot denials via pstore console-ramoops (if present)
checks.command: sh -c 'ls /sys/fs/pstore/console-ramoops* >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo NO_PSTORE; exit 0; }; c=$(cat /sys/fs/pstore/console-ramoops* 2>/dev/null | grep -ci "avc:"); echo "AVC_PSTORE_COUNT=$c"'
checks.description: Evidence: counts 'avc:' denials from previous boot if pstore is enabled.

checks.label: Android: live kernel log denials via /proc/kmsg (short sample)
checks.command: sh -c 'if [ -r /proc/kmsg ]; then (timeout 2 cat /proc/kmsg 2>/dev/null || true) | grep -i "avc:" | head -n 20; else echo NO_KMSG; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: samples kernel messages for 'avc:' (may require root; timeout avoids blocking).

checks.label: Linux: kernel log AVC count via journalctl (if available)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v journalctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then c=$(journalctl -k 2>/dev/null | grep -ci "avc:" ); echo "AVC_JOURNALCTL_COUNT=$c"; else echo NO_JOURNALCTL; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: counts AVC denials in kernel journal (availability depends on system logging).

checks.label: Linux: audit.log AVC count (if audit log exists)
checks.command: sh -c 'if [ -r /var/log/audit/audit.log ]; then c=$(grep -ci "avc:" /var/log/audit/audit.log 2>/dev/null); echo "AVC_AUDITLOG_COUNT=$c"; else echo NO_AUDIT_LOG; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: counts AVC denials in auditd log if present.

checks.label: Linux: ausearch recent AVC events (if ausearch exists)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v ausearch >/dev/null 2>&1; then ausearch -m avc -ts recent 2>/dev/null | head -n 40; else echo NO_AUSEARCH; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: shows recent AVC events via audit tooling (requires audit subsystem/logs).

checks.label: Linux: auditd service active (systemd systems)
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then systemctl is-active auditd 2>/dev/null || true; else echo NO_SYSTEMCTL; fi'
checks.description: Evidence: checks auditd status where systemd is used.

checks.label: Bootloader SELinux Override Not Permissive
checks.command: sh -c 'v=$(getprop ro.boot.selinux 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$v" ] || [ "$v" = "enforcing" ]; then echo OK; else echo "OVERRIDE=$v"; fi'
checks.description: ro.boot.selinux is set by the bootloader and can override runtime enforcing. If set to 'permissive', device is globally permissive regardless of getenforce output. Empty or 'enforcing' = safe.

checks.label: SELinux Enforce File Not World-Writable
checks.command: sh -c 'if [ -e /sys/fs/selinux/enforce ]; then perms=$(ls -la /sys/fs/selinux/enforce 2>/dev/null | awk "{print \$1}"); echo "$perms" | grep -qE '^.{8}[^-]' && echo WRITABLE || echo OK; else echo NO_FILE; fi'
checks.description: If /sys/fs/selinux/enforce is world-writable, any process can disable SELinux without root. Should be read-only for non-root. WRITABLE = critical misconfiguration.

checks.label: No Individual Permissive Domains
checks.command: sh -c 'if command -v semanage >/dev/null 2>&1; then out=$(semanage permissive -l 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$out" ] && echo CLEAN || echo "$out"; elif [ -r /sys/fs/selinux/policy ]; then # Android: check for permissive= in policy attributes via getprop out=$(getprop | grep -i permissive 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$out" ] && echo CLEAN || echo "$out"; else echo NO_TOOL; fi'
checks.description: A device can report global Enforcing mode while individual critical domains (e.g. untrusted_app, shell) are set permissive. semanage permissive -l lists them on Linux; getprop permissive hint on Android. NO_TOOL = tool not available, not a failure.
